I’ve played a lot of games over the years, but few collections have genuinely impressed me the way Halo: The Master Chief Collection has. What stands out to me most is not just the amount of content included, but the care, scale, and legacy behind it. It feels less like a normal game collection and more like a complete preservation of one of the most important franchises in gaming history.

What immediately impressed me was the sheer scope of the experience. Having multiple Halo titles brought together in one place already feels ambitious, but the way MCC allows me to move through different generations of Halo seamlessly is what makes it exceptional. I can go from the atmosphere and mystery of Halo: Combat Evolved to the emotional weight of Halo Reach, then into the larger-scale battles of Halo 3, all without feeling disconnected. It genuinely feels like a journey through gaming history.

Another thing that impressed me deeply is how much effort went into modernization without destroying the original identity of the games. The updated visuals are incredible, especially when switching between classic and remastered graphics. That feature alone constantly reminds me how advanced these games were for their time. Instead of replacing the originals, MCC respects them. It allows me to appreciate both the nostalgia and the technical improvements at the same time.

The multiplayer experience is another major reason why I admire MCC so much. The amount of maps, modes, customization options, and cross-game playlists creates almost endless replay value. It feels like a celebration of everything Halo multiplayer has ever represented — competitive gameplay, teamwork, creativity, and unforgettable moments with other players. Few collections manage to preserve online communities the way MCC has.

I’m also impressed by how the developers continued improving the collection over time. MCC had a difficult launch historically, but what matters to me is the commitment shown afterward. Instead of abandoning the project, it evolved into something genuinely respected. Continuous updates, quality-of-life improvements, PC support, mod support, Forge content, and expanded customization transformed it into one of the most complete FPS collections ever made. That level of dedication earns my respect because it shows long-term commitment to both the franchise and the community.

What makes MCC special to me personally is the emotional impact. Halo has always been more than just shooting mechanics or sci-fi storytelling. There’s a feeling attached to it — the music, the atmosphere, the sense of scale, the loneliness of certain missions, and the excitement of massive battles. MCC preserves all of that while making it accessible for both longtime fans and newer players. It reminds me why Halo became iconic in the first place.

I’m impressed by MCC because it succeeds in something very difficult: honoring the past while still feeling relevant today. It delivers nostalgia without relying only on nostalgia. It respects the original games while improving accessibility and presentation for modern audiences. Very few gaming collections achieve that balance as successfully as Halo MCC does.

Halo: The Master Chief Collection is a compilation of all the first person shooter Halo titles by Bungie, and Halo 4 by 343 Industries. These are story driven as much as they are gameplay driven experiences, and there is more than enough content here to make a Flood blush. 

You play as Master Chief, a man with the ability to use any weapon in existence, battling an army of angry aliens who hate humanity, and another army of parasitic lifeforms who hate absolutely everything. There's also an AI who looks a bit derpy since the programmers hadn't turned on the DLSS 5 filter yet, and after doing so has become a waifu we all can't help but goon over. 

Note: I am reviewing this purely on the basis of only playing the first title. 

Pros:

PC performance is excellent, with resolution going up to 4K, and a nice stable frame rate
Switching between old and new graphics is a nice touch. Really shows you how far graphics have come 
Music is as classic as it has ever been, with the Halo theme in particular being a standout 
Level variety is a mix of large open areas with more indoor sections
Enemy AI, to this day, is still some of the best in video games
Enemy variety is strong, with two factions that both play very differently to each other 
Enemy types have their own personalities depending on situation, with the Covenent grunts in particular being a favorite with how they'll run away with their arms in the air 
Combat is generally excellent, with a ton of dynamic elements, including grenade explosion chain reactions, which can also be to your own detriment 
The story is much more interesting than I remember it being
Level design is solid, with many opportunities for redirecting your attack or taking cover 
Weapon combos are almost essential on high difficulties, encouraging smart loadout choices 
Stealing a banshee and then raining hellfire from above is always a joy 
Enemies are mostly projectile based instead of hitscan, which makes them feel fair and balanced

Cons:

Starting the collection for the first time is a massive pain in the ass. Pop ups, notifications, news, forced sign in with a Microsoft account. It's a freaking mess! Am I playing a game or browsing a subscription service menu?
Some levels are reused, leading to a feeling of the game being smaller than it is 
The warthog controls like utter ass. It's far too floaty and has basically no weight to it 
The ghost also controls like ass and regularly gets hung up on environmental objects 
Checkpoint activation is dynamic but that can lead to frustration as you might get a checkpoint right before a swarm of enemies come charging in and you're not ready
The Flood are a fine enemy faction, but they are much less interesting than the Covenant when it comes to combat engagements 
The Forerunner terminals are a bunch of convoluted information that has zero context, though you can sort of work out what is being told to you 
Hunters are still able to be one-shot with a pistol, which makes them a heavy unit easier than a grunt

As someone who never played Halo up until now, I decided to get into this to know what the hype was all about. So far, I've only done Halo CE, 2, and 3 on Heroic.

For Halo CE, I really had a lot a fun playing it. How easily immersed I've been to the setting in the way the missions transition from breathtakingly free to eerily isolating is amazing. The backgrounds in the first half kept me exploring the map. Being able to ride different vehicles was great, and switching between different guns and the two grenades was fun. I didn't expect there to be some stealth sequences, but it led to some really funny moments of just whacking the sleeping Grunts and clueless Elites. The way this game introduced the Flood with the dead bodies was spectacular. Adnd that final warthog sequence cementing this game for me as "peak."

And then I went on to Halo 2, I thought, "I can see how much they could improve upon its gameplay, but how will the sequel write a better story?" And boy, did it surprise me. It lets you play as the enemy, which meant I could wield the Energy Sword (Arbiter was peak). And then you could dual-wield along with the new weapons. And then Sgt. Johnson kept throwing iconic lines left and right with his appearances. Spectacular follow-up, barring the underwhelming final boss and that cliffhanger ending. I wondered how the third installment will fare.

So then I went on to play the third Halo, which I was quite skeptical at first because it deducted the amount of grenades you could hold. But then I played more of the missions, and it led to me ask, "what is this game and why is it one of the most thrilling experiences after doing it twice with the last two halo games?" More missile weapons, flying vehicles, exploding scarabs in the actual gameplay and not just transitioning to a cutscene of them exploding, the story coming to a full circle by resolving all the character arcs and doing another Halo CE-final sequence with the epic music and the piano— what the heck was this game??

This was an amazing experience, and I hope ODST and Reach will continue to be amazing experiences.

a handsome collection of every mainline halo game since 2001, all.. well, [i]somewhat neatly[/i] packaged into one very fairly priced bundle. while the master chief collection was absurdly broken for its original console release a little over a decade ago, almost all of the issues therein have since been rectified (some very minor problems and inaccuracies from the original releases do remain, however; such as the ai behavior tick rate, and many barely noticeable graphical errors).

despite that, i do still consider this to be the definitive way to run through all of the old halo titles. it boasts many major graphical improvements over the originals, such as support for much higher framerates, display resolutions, and improved graphical fidelity overall thanks to the two included "anniversary" editions (though many in the community consider combat evolved anniversary's remastered visuals to be unfaithful, as they fundamentally misunderstand the tone, atmosphere, and art style of the original game). while many publishers will simply dump their "remastered" slop onto the market and take the original releases to the chopping block, this game does not do that. you can seamlessly transition from the original and remastered editions of the game at will, with the push of a button [i]while[/i] you are actively playing, which is an amazing feature i don't think i've ever seen another game do.

they also don't bloat the hell out of the game with asinine microtransactions, which is a HUGE plus in the current climate of the games industry. you are free to peruse the game's supplementary progression system at your own leisure; whether that be through the singleplayer campaigns, or the frantic hellscape that is the multiplayer. in doing so, you unlock sweet little cosmetic goodies that you can use to personalize your gameplay experience. additionally, they've also recently made permanently available [i]most[/i] of the seasonal cosmetics from "the exchange", that you previously had to wait several weeks or months for to retrieve, which is quite generous. and, to top it all off, there is also extensive modding support available for every title via the steam workshop, allowing you to spice up the game in numerous ways, which adds nigh infinite fun and replayability. all in all, a fantastic experience all around. you certainly get your money's worth, even while off-sale.

You need a Microsoft account to play?? Really??? Wish i saw that before i purchased it.
Screw useless accounts and setups. I want to play a game, not create another stupid account for no good reason.
Halo used to be good, the port was a bit buggy on release but improved over time, though it is still a bit off

What an insane experience

100%ing Halo MCC had to of been the toughest thing in gaming I've ever had to do and it required an incredible amount of knowledge, skill and game sense not just in 1 game but in all 6 different Halo games. Most were easy but some of the more difficult ones were Multiplayer achievements, Campaign difficulty/scores/par-times, collectibles and many more but the ultimate test was LASO (Legendary All Skulls On)

The biggest test was completing 6 different LASO where I had to play on the hardest difficulty with multiple in-game modifiers like increasing enemy rank/shields/awareness, enemies throw more grenades, health only recharges on melee and like 12 other ones. The skill gap was insane and it required me to break the game to even get through this challenge.

I had to learn techniques like butterflying, Shield/Flood bumping, out of map traversing, A.I duplicating, grenade stacks, bridge fall, warhog flinging, hammer launching, head glitching, Oracle Invincibility, fall damage cancelling and many more all while playing with a friend and his 60 year old dad (Except for Halo CE and 2). The longest LASO map was Gravemind being a 6 hour straight slug fest with 2 hours of that being learning how to butterfly off a brute for Prison skip. Despite all that there were many bug/glitches we couldnt do like, B.O.O.L, Belly Skip, Shield Teleport and Butterflying on High Charity, those are definitely reserved for some of the best of the best in Halo 

Now it's time to wait for Campaign Evolved and do it over again. The biggest help came from I.V. gamin', Halo Completionist and Jervalin.
Requiring a Microsoft account verification every time you want to play is bonkers.

This is going to be a long review so if you don't want to read it all I don't blame, but if you read the whole review I appreciate it.

Halo CE/A-
For being the first halo it was basic, simple, A to B missions, and perfect. The combat is what you would expect from a 2001 although Halo CE was more advance. The Guns are good not a single one is bad and there broken in multiplayer. The enemies are interesting, but when it comes to an elite in a ghost or a flood with a rocket launcher the game takes a little challenging turn. the vehicles are what makes halo so unique, the warthog is so clean and the best vehicle to be introduced for in a 2001 modern futuristic alien shooter, the ghost are in CE are okay I'd only take one if its the only vehicle near by, the tank (Scorpion) should speak for its self blows everything in its way only problem is it doesn't have that much health compared to the other Halos, and the last drivable vehicle would be the banshee, its like the ghost as in its an okay vehicle but not really fun to use. The story is amazing with learning about the Halos, the covenant and there religious beliefs. Over all I give it a 9/10 recommendation.

Halo 2/2A-
Halo 2 was a huge step up from Halo CE as its slightly similar to CE there is a lot more content and lore. The combat is still the same but now you can duel wield smgs and pistols. Halo 2 introduced some new guns such as the smg, battle rifle, and a Machine turret which could be picked up. The new covenant weapons are carbines, beam rifle, brute plasma rifle, brute shot, and the energy sword which was in Halo CE but was not usable. The enemies are the same from Ce, but there are 3 new enemies such as the brutes, drones, and the Prophets who are the rulers of the covenant. The vehicles were the same only added one new drivable vehicle which is the spectre which is the covenant version of the warthog, its not a bad vehicle when your playing as the arbiter. The lore was a headache with how much you learn about the human covenant war and the reasoning in why they want power of the Halo rings. this is the only game ill bring up the difficulties. If you have balls to play on legendary good luck. The jackals will kindly introduce you to the beam rifle. Over all I give it a 10/10 recommendation.

Halo 3-
After the cliff hanger at the end of Halo 2, Halo 3 is the great journey after the events of Halo 2. Combat had a slight bit of change because now you can pick up abilities, defenses, emps, heals, and flashbangs that explode. The weapons had some changes the new weapons are spikers, maulers, spartan lasers, gravity hammers, missiles pods, and flamethrowers. The enemies are the same, but there is a species that is now longer in the covenant but instead teaming up with you and the rest of the UNSC. There are 7 new vehicles the mongoose which is a ATV that hold up to 2 people is kind of a mid vehicle, the hornet is a flying vehicle that is super fun to drive its holds up to 3 people, the troop transport warthog is just a warthog without its turret and instead holds 5 people, its not a bad vehicle but I wouldn't ride it into combat, the elephant is a massive mobile UNSC base that you can drive. It can carry other vehicles, its a decent vehicle but is just very slow, the brute chopper is like the banshee but the front is very dangerous so don't let it ram you, the brute prowler is the same as the Halo 2 spectre, the AA wraith is an anti-aircraft vehicle pretty good and fun to use. The lore is still a headache but it becomes more understanding and once you finish the game you understand it. Over all I give it a 8/10 recommendation.

Halo 3 ODST- 
Halo 3 ODST is during the events of Halo 2. The combat is similar to the other Halos. There is a change in weapons as there is a new smg and a new awful pistol. The enemies are the same as halo 3's enemies. The Vehicles are also the same. The lore is the only thing different, you're working with your ODST squad and playing as them in separate missions will staying on an objective that is top secret and only your squad captain know the mission the real mission at least. Over all I give it a 7.5/10 recommendation.

Halo Reach-
Halo Reach took place right before Halo CE and it was perfect and absolutely amazing plus the last Halo we got from Bungie. The combat has new abilities which are armor abilities such as sprint, jet packs, armor lock, active camouflage, hologram, drop shield, and evade. They are very fun to use just know they don't last forever. The basic weapons are there which is the assault rifles, magnums, shotguns, and rocket launcher. I'm not gonna name every covenant weapon that's in the other halos. Halo Reach gave us 8 new weapons such as The DMR, grenade launcher, target locator, needle rifle, plasma repeater, focus rifle, and the plasma launcher. These guns are very fun to use not a single thing bad about them. There are some new enemies variants like the skirmisher jackals, engineers, and the Gútas. There are 3 new vehicles such as the Falcon, Sabre, and the revenant. There is 1 new warthog variants which is the Rocket Warthog. The lore is so good and full of surprises, there is so many unexpected turn of events that can happen behind you, above you, or an explosion that catches you off guard, even a pelican can have some mass to your experience, and if you feel out numbered just keep fighting until you're MIA. Over all I give halo Reach a 11/10 recommendation.

Halo 4- 
Well well well look what 343 handed us. Halo 4 isn't terrible it just shouldn't have been made, I'd be happier. The combat is the same. Same basic guns with new ones that I'm not naming. The enemies are the same and there is a new species called the Prometheans there boring to fight. Same vehicles from the other good Halos, but you can drive a pelican for the first time in a mission. The lore is so bad I forgot most of it already even though I just played all the Halos before this review. All I know is the evil Forunner wants to blow up earth and you got to stop him by throwing him in a bug zapper lame fight and ending too. Over all I give it a 4/10 recommendation.

Even though these 2 Halos aren't on the MCC I'm still gonna review them

Halo infinite- 
Halo infinite was like seeing a new Call of Duty Trailer thinking its gonna be good. Halo infinite has a lot new vehicles, guns, and enemies. I don't have a problem with any of them there not bad, but I like sticking to the basics. The enemies are under a new leader and are called the banished. The open world experience in my opinion is okay, but when you beat the main story and side missions there's just nothing you want to do next. The lore is okay definitely better than Halo 4's lore. Over all I give Halo infinite a 5.5/10 recommendation.

Halo 5-
-10/10 recommendation.

Only other Halos are the Halo Wars which is a different branch from the other Halos so I'm not gonna review them.

Loved Halo back when Combat Evolved came out in 2001. I remember all of the LAN parties we had for Halo 1 and 2 back in high school. Getting this game was a great way to bring back the nostalgia for one one of my favorite games from my youth while giving it a modern coat of paint as well as giving me an opportunity to play the other sequel/prequel games I didn't play when they originally came out. It's also a huge plus that the game works very well on the Steam Deck.

All this said, the Windows sign-in feature is a huge pain deal with. I already logged into Steam, why can't that be enough authentication that I bought the game? To make matters worse, as of today there seems to be some kind of bug making the sign-in authentication not work. This means that despite the extra step of having to sign-in a second time, I can't even play my game. Pretty ridiculous.

An absolute masterpiece of a collection and quite frankly, one of the greatest value propositions in all of PC gaming. This is the ultimate gaming time capsule in my opinon.

It’s no secret that the collection had a incredibly rough borderline shocking launch years ago. But the developers stuck with it and after years of meticulous updates and PC optimization with feature additions, the Master Chief Collection stands today as a flawless triumph ngl. You are getting six legendary games (usually at a quite frank steal when on sale), fully remastered with cross-play, Forge, and a unified multiplayer ecosystem for less than the price of a single modern AAA title.

Here is how the legendary saga breaks down:

>>>The Games<<<
HALO: COMBAT EVOLVED ANNIVERSARY: The blueprint that changed the industry forever. The sense of isolation on the mysterious ring world, the brilliant enemy AI and that iconic orchestral score still hit just as hard today. The ability to swap between the classic graphics and the remastered visuals with a single button press is a brilliant touch even if the classic atmosphere remains unmatched.

HALO 2: ANNIVERSARY: This is the gold standard of what a remaster should be in my opinion; the rewritten cutscenes by Blur Studio are breathtakingly beautiful looking like a modern sci-fi film. Mechanically it introduces dual-wielding and splits the narrative between the Master Chief and the Arbiter, expanding the lore of the Covenant in a deeply engaging way.

HALO 3: The grand finale of the original trilogy and a masterclass in sandbox design. Every level feels like an epic sweeping battlefield. The gunplay, the vehicles and the environmental storytelling culminate in one of the most satisfying conclusions to a gaming trilogy ever conceived.

HALO 3: ODST: Honestly a brilliant atmospheric shift in perspective. Dropping into the neon-drenched streets of New Mombasa as a vulnerable rookie rather than a Spartan changes the pacing entirely. Backed by a phenomenal, melancholy jazz soundtrack, it’s a beautifully distinct entry in the franchise.

HALO 4: While the gameplay and art direction shift away from the classic Bungie style, it features an incredibly emotional deep dive into the relationship between Master Chief and Cortana. Visually stunning, even if the new enemy factions feel a bit more tedious to fight.

HALO REACH: A masterpiece of a prequel and Bungie's tragic beautiful send-off to the franchise... Because you already know the ultimate fate of Planet Reach, every mission carries a heavy, emotional weight. The campaign is gritty, intense, and features some of the best level design in the entire series.

>>>Performance & Multiplayer<<<
On the technical side the port job is fantastic and well optimized. The collection runs incredibly smooth hitting high frame rates on my system.
The multiplayer is a dream for preservation, instead of fracturing the community, the matchmaking system allows you to mix and match game modes and maps from across all six titles seamlessly. Whether you want the chaotic fun of Halo CE's physics, the competitive sweat of Halo 2, or the endless creativity of Halo 3’s custom games and Forge maps, you will find active lobbies and endless replayability.

>>>The Downside<<< :/
The only real blemish on the package is the mandatory Microsoft/Xbox Live account login..., which can occasionally be a minor hassle to sync up on Steam but it's a small price to pay for the sheer amount of content provided.

>>>Final Verdict<<<
Whether you are looking to tackle six legendary co-op campaigns with a friend, lose hundreds of hours chasing rare achievements or dive into an active multiplayer sandbox that respects your time and wallet, the Master Chief Collection is a must-own.
They truly don't make games like this anymore at this price.

Highly recommended, a solid 117/10.
Forcing Microsoft account credentials on a games developed on the OG xbox and years after online purchase is wild and sadly expected from such an anti consumer company.

multiplayer is just filled with millenials who can't hack it anymore or just people in general that resort to cheating to give themselves an ego boost. not worth even trying if you're looking for old school multiplayer. say this as someone that is good at FPS's. it's just limp ♥♥♥♥♥ stroking their ego.

It is great to have the original Halo Trilogy all in one place. 343 Industries remastered the first two games and I have split opinions about each. Regarding Halo: CE, the remaster misses the mark in most ways. It fails to capture the mood and lighting of the original areas like the Library and Truth and Reconciliation. I preferred to play the original with the classic graphics. On the other hand, Halo 2's remaster is fantastic. It improves upon it in every single way. The only issue I experienced with my PC, a Linux based iMac, was characters popping in and out and some inconsistency with the say the Sentinels explode versus what you should be seeing on screen. For example, in the original, when you destroy a sentinel they fall to the ground and explode. If you are nearby they will do damage to your shields or kill you. In the remaster there is a visual inconsistency where you don't see them explode on the ground but yet they do damage to your shield. Definitely a strange thing to miss in the remaster. Otherwise I think that the package is a great way to experience the OG Bungie games if that is what you are looking for.

If you get this collection just keep in mind that Microsoft/Xbox has it's own sign in when launching the game. The reason I am giving a negative review is I can't even stay signed in while in-game. I have 30s to a minute before it signs me back out to the start screen. A loop.

Growing up, I wasn't much of a "Halo kid"; and having only completed the series on Heroic since owning MCC, I still felt rather incomplete as a fan of these games. But having recently completed my first Halo game (Reach) on Legendary I can definitely say that I have become a "Halo kid"! Halo CE, 2, 3 and Reach are four of the best campaigns for any series of games; they're wonderfully paced and satisfying on higher difficulties!

I'm not much for multiplayer in general (kinda play games to be away from other people for a bit!), but I have definitely managed a few fun, if only respectable matches across the games. Having pretty much missed the boat on Halo 2/Halo 3 multiplayer back in the day - so no point crying about it!
I love how they combined all the games and made it into 1 simple game with an efficient launcher.
Classic Halo experience, some of the best games ever made. Great value.
unplayable with bluetooth headphones/headsets. Game changes channel to call mode for headphones after accessing the main menu. apparently issue is known for some years now but never tried to fix.

one of the best series you will find in gaming with amazing gameplay that only gets better as you progress from one title to the next while also offering a really injoyble story with a fleshed out graphics design that has left its mark as the iconic first person shooter Halo

The games play surprisingly well? The AI is quite advanced compared to games of its era - enemies dodge grenades, push environmental obstacles to get to you. The music is good. The story is intriguing. I didn't have high expectations since old games didn't really put the effort to be great and were just good enough but it seems the devs of these games really put in the extra hours. Pleasantly surprised.

HALO is just so good, and the Master Chief Collection I'm so glad is something that exists, because it preserves these classics and makes them more accessible to anyone, whether they've played HALO forever or are just getting into it. I'm not someone who's a big fan of playing shooters on the controller, I personally prefer the keyboard, but since HALO was designed specifically with controller in mind it feels great even for me. It's GREAT on the steam deck and it should be verified ngl. HALO's story is so well conveyed through the gameplay that you don't really need to look into extra things like the shows or the books just to fall in love with the characters. Unfortunately, it's one of those things that are old enough to where everyone assumes everyone else knows what happens already, like how everyone knows that Luke is Darth Vader's son without even watching Star Wars, and so it's really easy to get spoiled.

It was an inside job! bush did 343!

Halo is a great game with fun combat, memorable weapons, and an excellent atmosphere. However, the campaign suffers from too many repetitive sections and reused environments, which can make parts of the game feel longer than they need to be. At times, the repetition becomes tiring, and you find yourself just wanting the level to be over.
Great game and definitely worth the price. It has an excellent campaign. 10/10.

Great to revisit for the campaigns. Reach is easily one of the best games in the FPS genre.

Every single Halo game from CE to Reach is just ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ fantastic, and this collection comes with every single one. I'm literally getting the chills! Did you feel that?! 🤓😤😵‍💫🫨🤓

This is a collection of a series that is near and dear to millions of hearts, even for full price you can't go wrong. I recommended it for obvious reasons but if I'd give it stars, I'd give a 4 not 5 just because when this game came out it was utterly broken for a solid year and while it's miles better now it can still have issues like crashing. Most of the games within the master chief collection get a 5 star easily (4 stars for halo 4), for Halo 1-3, ODST, and REACH are all certified bangers, and matchmaking and doing campaign playlists across all games here on the MCC is quite the experience. If it was more solid, especially at launch, I'd give a 5 star but oh well. Even for the full $40 its worth it for the 6 games (under $7 a game!), let alone the sale price of $10 (under $2 a game!) Pick it up if you love shooters and/or sci-fi and especially pick it up if you played halo years ago but haven't in awhile! It's a dream come true to play all the halo games in one place matchmaking with friends instead of having to game hop, talking to people in game chat like the good old days of Xbox 360 where these games were at their prime, and playing with high frame rates and settings on modern hardware where the action feels so much more responsive and life-like! Pick it now! Here's to keeping the nostalgia alive and the many gaming memories to come! See you on the battlefield spartans!
The most efficient way to buy nostalgia, and a ton of good content for the price
Oi, top notches legend'ry story mate. Gameplay unmatched like water bill mate. mate.

Wonderful collection if you're a fan of the series. They did a great job with this, really. It's not a quick money-grab - the amount of content contained in this collection is a little crazy if you think about. Personally Halo 1 and Halo 3 are all time favorite action games. This is an easy recommend if you like Halo or like FPS's and want a ton of content for the asking price.

Never expected to fall back in love with the game, but it just never gets old!

I mean its halo, lets say you wasn't there for the golden xbox 360 era; now you can relive it. Every campaign is worth enjoying 10 times over (except for halo 4, once or twice is good enough). My only issue is with playing co-op campaign online it can run a little laggy if you are not the host which is really unfortunate, but still great fun reliving the campaign with my brother. I would say 343 did a good job over the years keeping this game alive, especially regarding the free cosmetics. Once you buy the game and its dlcs you'll never spend a dime again on this game. On a final note, once you master each campaign; go for the LASO run, you'll have so much fun.
I don't know what to tell you, I love this game, but most of the time, it just does not work! Always on Live Stream too!

No matter how much you report players nothing happens. The griefing seems like its non stop, especially with players who want to take your sniper or rocket launcher. The only aspect of this game that's enjoyable is the campaing.
